killed.description: One-line reason a superseded topic died — the why-we-didn't is worth keeping. Shown in the Graveyard strip.

focus.description: At most ONE topic may carry focus: true — the thing actually being touched now (capacity-of-one shop). Validator warns on duplicates.

history.description: Real dates for work that predates git-tracking, so the date-progression tracker can back-date it on the timeline/rewind. `designed` = when it was designed/planned; `built` = when it was finished. YYYY-MM-DD. `builtApprox: true` marks a derived/estimated built date (never invent a precise one — flag it).

deps.description: PRODUCT ordering (this topic requires that one), kind hard|chosen with a why. Distinct from Agora plan 'after' (execution scheduling within one wave) — never unify or derive one from the other. See tools/agora/PLANNING-STACK.md §4.

feature.description: Optional edge-to-member: the slug (base slug() of the feature's title) of a specific feature inside the target topic. Present = the dependant requires that one feature, not the whole topic. Validator checks it resolves to a real feature of the dep target.

open.description: Mirror of the linked subspec's '## Open' bullet count (settledness axis). Counted from the doc, never guessed; recompute when the doc changes.

spike.description: true = research that could invalidate the approach (measure-don't-assume, unsolved design question) — rendered dashed with ⚠ so it doesn't masquerade as a build step.

parallel.description: true = this step does NOT wait for the previous step; it branches straight off the parent tile instead of chaining. Absent/false = sequential (default): the step chains off the one before it. Only affects the plan-map's step-tile arrows.

history.description: Real designed/built dates for a step that predates git-tracking (same shape as the topic-level `history`). YYYY-MM-DD.
